title: 7 Settlement of ICBC Agents芒聙聶 Accounts
body_text:
7	Settlement of ICBC Agents芒聙聶 Accounts
7.1	Summary of ICBC Agents芒聙聶 Account Settlement
Settlement of Cheques Processed in Branch Capture
Central 1 credits your financial institution芒聙聶s Central 1 account for the value of cheques processed in Branch Capture. Branch Capture deposits appear on your financial institution芒聙聶s statement (AIBC/AIBU for BC, AIOC/AIOU for Ontario) as code 芒聙聹BC芒聙聺.
Central 1 may issue a deposit adjustment for cheques processed via Branch Capture if
an item was deposited that can芒聙聶t be processed (e.g., image quality problems, piggybacks);
an item amount was keyed incorrectly and a deposit was force-balanced; or
an item was received that shouldn芒聙聶t have been included in the deposit (e.g., the currency of the item doesn芒聙聶t match the currency of the deposit type, the item isn芒聙聶t eligible for clearing).
See the Deposit Processing Part for more information on deposit adjustments.
Settlement for ICBC Agent芒聙聶s Accounts
When Central 1 initiates the sweep, an ICBC agent芒聙聶s account is debited for the deposit total(s) that you report on the ICBC Deposit Slip in ServiceNow the following day by pre-authorized debit (PAD). These transactions appear on the Automated Transfer System Detailed Listing (CCD) report. The account balance should be zero at the end of each processing cycle.
You must monitor and reconcile ICBC agent芒聙聶s accounts daily. If there is a problem with a pre-authorized debit (PAD), don芒聙聶t return it through the clearing. If an error is identified, submit a Paper Payments and Cash Returns Trace Request form in ServiceNow. See Section 6.2 Managing Deposit Errors for an ICBC Agent芒聙聶s Account, for procedures.
END
